threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the Tube app every morning; a local knows it’s just noise. Real-time updates? More like real-time frustration.
Last Friday, some bloke was trying to sell homemade curry on the 59. Said it was 'better than any takeaway'. Classic Brixton hustler.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a group of kids arguing over which Döner shop serves the best sauce; that’s when I realized this place feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you sip your morning coffee at a former DDR prison that’s now a cozy café. History and caffeine blend perfectly here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know the best taco truck spot is under the freeway, not the ones just off 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy in a suit argue with a pigeon at the stop yesterday. Pretty sure the bird won, he just walked away shaking his head.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can stroll into a bar and get a vermut any time. But around here, that's strictly a Sunday ritual. They miss the vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by their map-fumbling at the Diagonal, still unsure if ‘Torre’ means tower or something else altogether.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube' instead of the 'Overground' when you hop on at Dalston Junction. That's a rookie mistake, mate.
Caught the N35 last night; a guy was singing Motown classics at the top of his lungs. Turns out he was on his way to a sixtieth birthday party. Pure vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: You know you're in Neukölln when the kebab shop owner knows your order by heart and the sound of U-Bahn trains mixed with street music feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can get a döner kebab served with a side of lavas bread and homemade spicy sauce after midnight. Just a regular Friday night here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they react when the BART stops at 16th and Mission. The eye roll is instantaneous, like clockwork.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus today, and a guy with three parrots rolled on. Just another day in the Tenderloin, where the weird is normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's squares are just for paella and selfies, but locals actually treasure the evening chats over a single coffee, not a full meal.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their awkward order at the bakery, fumbling with 'un croissant' instead of just saying 'un.'
